about.title: About
about.description: Learn about my background in Bioengineering and systems-focused engineering.
about.evolutionFromDetail: Biological system modeling, signal processing, and experimental rigor.
about.evolutionToDetail: Backend, platform engineering, and distributed systems architecture.
about.mindsetTitle: How I think as an engineer
about.mindsetDescription: The same principles I applied in the lab guide how I design, validate, and operate software in production.

mindsetItems.title: Systems thinking
mindsetItems.bioContext: I analyze interactions between variables, feedback loops, and emergent effects in biological systems.
mindsetItems.softwareContext: I design services with clear boundaries, explicit contracts, and conscious dependencies.

mindsetItems.title: Complex system modeling
mindsetItems.bioContext: I build models that simplify reality without losing the variables that matter.
mindsetItems.softwareContext: I translate business domains into modular, event-driven, scalable architectures.

mindsetItems.title: Experimental validation
mindsetItems.bioContext: I formulate hypotheses, design controlled experiments, and measure reproducible results.
mindsetItems.softwareContext: I automate tests, CI/CD, and reproducible environments before every deploy.

mindsetItems.title: Evidence-driven design
mindsetItems.bioContext: I make decisions based on data, not intuition — especially with noisy signals.
mindsetItems.softwareContext: I prioritize observability, business metrics, and SLOs to guide iterations.

interestDescriptions.ai: Exploring models that amplify human capabilities — from applied ML to agents.
interestDescriptions.quantum: Interest in quantum algorithms and their future impact on optimization and cryptography.
interestDescriptions.neuro: A natural bridge between biological signals and distributed processing systems.
interestDescriptions.fitness: Measurable discipline: incremental progress, consistency, and variable optimization.

about.bioBridgeDescription: My scientific background directly shapes how I design, validate, and operate software systems in production.
bioBridgeTooltips: The rigor of biological signal processing translates into robust data pipelines and real-time event streams.
Modeling complex biological systems is equivalent to designing microservices with clear boundaries and low coupling.
Experimental validation in the lab is the same mindset behind automated testing and CI/CD.
Statistical analysis of noisy data becomes observability, metrics, and performance analysis.
